title: Pothole / Road Damage
citizen_steps:
1. Take a clear photo of the pothole from different angles.
2. Note the exact location (landmark, street, nearby building).
3. Avoid standing in the middle of traffic while capturing photos.
4. Submit a complaint with details, including how long the issue exists.
5. Share the complaint ID and follow up if not resolved in a few days.
authority_steps:
1. Log the complaint with unique ticket ID.
2. Inspect site and estimate repair material and manpower.
3. Schedule road cutting, base filling and asphalt laying.
4. Barricade and display work‑in‑progress signboards.
5. Update complaint status once work is completed.

title: Garbage / Solid Waste
citizen_steps:
1. Take photos showing uncollected garbage or overflowing bins.
2. Separate your own household waste into dry and wet to reduce load.
3. Lodge a complaint with date, time and frequency of the problem.
4. Avoid burning waste; it causes pollution and health problems.
5. Encourage neighbours to store waste in bags for easy collection.
authority_steps:
1. Assign nearest sanitation worker / vehicle to the location.
2. Clear accumulated garbage and disinfect the area.
3. Check regular collection schedule and optimise route.
4. Educate local residents on proper disposal points.
5. Monitor the area for repeated non‑collection complaints.

title: Sewage Overflow / Drain Block
citizen_steps:
1. Avoid contact with the sewage water to prevent infection.
2. Capture photos or a short video showing the overflow.
3. Report foul smell, duration and whether it entered homes/shops.
4. Keep children away from the affected area.
5. If water is entering your house, move valuables to higher places.
authority_steps:
1. Send field staff to clear blockages and open manholes safely.
2. Use jetting/sucking machines if required.
3. Disinfect streets after clearing sewage.
4. Inspect pipelines for structural damage.
5. Record root cause (clogging, broken pipe, illegal connection) and plan prevention.

title: Waterlogging / Flooding
citizen_steps:
1. Stay away from open manholes and electric poles in flooded areas.
2. If water enters your home, switch off main power if it is safe to do so.
3. Take photos of water level and note how long it remains.
4. File a complaint clearly mentioning nearest junction and colony name.
5. Avoid driving two‑wheelers through deep water.
authority_steps:
1. Deploy dewatering pumps at low‑lying points.
2. Clear blocked stormwater drains and inlets.
3. Coordinate with traffic police for diversions.
4. Plan long‑term measures like new drains or road re‑grading.
5. Communicate expected resolution time to citizens.

title: Streetlight / Electricity Issue
citizen_steps:
1. Note the exact pole number or closest house/shop number.
2. Observe whether the light flickers, stays off, or is broken.
3. Avoid touching poles, cables or junction boxes.
4. File complaint mentioning time when lights should normally be ON.
5. For major sparks/fire, call emergency helpline immediately.
authority_steps:
1. Register complaint and mark location on maintenance map.
2. Send technician to check bulb, driver, wiring and timers.
3. Replace faulty components and ensure power is safely restored.
4. Record repeated failure cases for infrastructure upgrade.
5. Update resolution time and closure notes in the system.

title: Other Civic Issue
citizen_steps:
1. Describe the problem as clearly as possible with photos.
2. Mention impact on safety, health or movement.
3. Provide nearest landmark and typical time when problem occurs.
4. Keep complaint ID safe for future reference.
5. In emergencies, contact local police or disaster helpline directly.
authority_steps:
1. Triage complaint and route to correct department.
2. Communicate expected timeline to the citizen.
3. If outside current scope, escalate to higher authority.
4. Coordinate between departments if multiple services are affected.
5. Document issue type to improve future routing and planning.
